data.title: Taco Bell is the star for Yum! Brands again in Q1
data.description:
Yum! Brands (YUM) slipped in early trading on Wednesday after posting its first-quarter earnings report.

Worldwide system sales grew 6% for the quarter that ended on March 31, excluding foreign currency translation, with KFC up 6% and Taco Bell 10% higher. Pizza Hut sales were flat for the quarter. The restaurant operator also pointed to strong digital system sales, approaching $11B with a record digital mix of 63%.

Overall, comparable sales rose 3% to top the consensus expectation of +2.7%. Same-store sales growth was positive for Taco Bell (+8%) and KFC (+2%) but was flat for Pizza Hut. The Habit Burger Grill Division's same-store sales were up 5% for the quarter. 

On the development front, unit growth was up 5% for the quarter, including 1,030 gross new units.

Yum! Brands' (YUM) core operating profit rose 6% across the company, led by the Taco Bell division. Adjusted EPS was reported at $1.50 vs. $1.39 consensus and $1.30 a year ago.

"Taco Bell delivered an outstanding 8% same-store sales growth, meaningfully ahead of the QSR industry, building off a very strong Q1 same-store sales growth rate in 2025. KFC delivered impressive unit growth and resilient same-store growth, with many KFC markets growing system sales in double-digits. Yum! is incredibly well positioned to sustain sales momentum thanks to strong global consumer appeal for our brands, long-term consumption tailwinds, and our tech and AI capabilities," highlighted CEO Chris Turner.

Shares of Yum! Brands (YUM) edged 0.3% lower in premarket trading on Tuesday to $156.46 vs. the 52-week range of $137.33 to $169.39.

data.title: Oatly GAAP EPS of -$0.38 beats by $0.55, revenue of $228.33M beats by $13.3M
data.description:
• Oatly press release (OTLY): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.38 beats by $0.55.
  • Revenue of $228.33M (+15.6% Y/Y) beats by $13.3M.
  • First quarter revenue of $228.3 million, a 15.6% increase compared to the prior year period, with a constant currency revenue increase of 8.1% compared to the prior year period.
  • The Company is, whilst navigating the visible impact from the conflict in the Middle East, reiterating its 2026 outlook as follows:
    • Constant currency revenue growth is expected to be in the range of +3% to +5%;
    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $25 million to $35 million; and
    • Capital expenditures are expected to be in the range of $20 million to $30 million.

data.title: Luckin Coffee Non-GAAP EPS of $0.32, revenue of $1.74B
data.description:
• Luckin Coffee press release (LKNCY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.32.
  • Revenue of $1.74B.
  • Total net revenues were RMB11,995.5 million (US$1,735.6 million), representing a 35.3% year-over-year increase.
  • Net new store openings were 2,548, comprising 2,531 stores in China (including in Hong Kong), one store in Singapore, 13 stores in Malaysia and 3 stores in U.S. Total number of stores increased to 33,596 at the first quarter end, comprising 21,807 self-operated stores and 11,789 partnership stores, which represented a store unit growth of 8.2% from the total store count as of the end of fourth quarter of 2025.
  • Average monthly transacting customers were 93.1 million, representing a 25.3% year-over-year increase.
  • Revenues from self-operated stores were RMB8,592.1 million (US$1,243.1 million), representing a 32.6% year-over-year increase.
  • Same-store sales growth for self-operated stores was negative 0.1%, compared to 9.2% in the same quarter of 2025.
  •

data.title: Garmin Non-GAAP EPS of $2.08 beats by $0.24, revenue of $1.75B beats by $40M
data.description:
• Garmin press release (GRMN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.08 beats by $0.24.
  • Revenue of $1.75B (+13.6% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • We are maintaining our fiscal year 2026 guidance of approximately $7.9 billion  vs $7.98B consensus revenue and pro forma EPS of $9.35 vs $9.39 consensus
  • Shares -2.2% PM.

data.title: Etsy GAAP EPS of $0.60 misses by $0.02, revenue of $631M beats by $13.54M
data.description:
• Etsy press release (ETSY): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.60 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $631M (-3.1% Y/Y) beats by $13.54M.
  • Etsy marketplace GMS was $2.5 billion, up 5.5% year-over-year, representing a 540 basis-points (bps)improvement compared to GMS growth in the fourth quarter of 2025. On a currency neutral basis, GMSgrowth was up 3.6% year-over-year. Progress in both product development and marketing are beginning totranslate into underlying improvements across marketplace fundamentals, and we also benefited from foreignexchange tailwinds and softer performance in the prior-year comparable period.
  •  
  • Shares +2.8% PM.

data.title: Amarin GAAP EPS of -$0.03 misses by $0.04, revenue of $45.1M beats by $1.33M
data.description:
• Amarin press release (AMRN): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.03 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $45.1M (+7.3% Y/Y) beats by $1.33M.

  • Cash: Reported aggregate cash and investments rose to $307.8 million as of March 31, 2026 compared to $302.6 million as of December 31, 2025.

  • Operating Loss: Narrowed to $11.3 million from an operating loss of $16.8 million, an improvement of $5.4 million, or 32%. Operating loss in Q1 2026 included restructuring costs of $3.3 million compared to no such charges in Q1 2025.

data.title: Teva Pharmaceutical beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Teva Pharmaceutical press release (TEVA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $4B (+2.8% Y/Y) beats by $210M.
  • Cash flow used in operating activities of $40 million
  • Free cash flow of $188 million.

  • 2026 Business Outlook maintained; updated exclusively for Emalex transaction:

    • Revenues of $16.4 - $16.8 billion vs. consensus of $16.50B
    • Non-GAAP operating income of $3.80 – $4.0 billion ($4.55 - $4.8 billion stand-alone), impacted by an expected $700 million IPR&D charge and $75 million to reflect Emalex’s operating expenses and transaction-related expenses.
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $4.23 – $4.53 billion ($5.0 - $5.3 billion stand-alone)
    • Non-GAAP diluted EPS of $1.91 – $2.11 ($2.57 - $2.77 stand-alone) vs. consensus of $2.68
    • Free cash flow of $2.0 - $2.4 billion

  •

data.title: Babcock & Wilcox books $21M in U.S. fuel-switching projects
data.description:
• Babcock & Wilcox (BW) on Wednesday said it has secured more than $21M in new awards for fuel-switching projects in the United States, saying demand for power supports use of coal and natural gas plants.
  • The company said the contracts cover engineering and material supply for projects that convert coal-fired boilers to natural gas and enable multi-fuel capability
  • The work builds on a previously completed 100-megawatt coal-to-gas conversion project in the fourth quarter of 2025. B&W added it is currently executing about 1200 megawatts of additional conversion projects expected to be completed this year.

data.title: Victoria's Secret rallies after BofA points to sales momentum
data.description:
Bank of America upgraded Victoria's Secret (VSCO) to a Buy rating from Neutral on its view that the retailer's sales momentum will continue

Analyst Mary Sport highlighted that Victoria's Secret (VSCO) has been able to gain market share in the intimates categories and thinks declines in the U.S. lingerie sector are moderating. "We think continued sales growth will result in significant operating margin expansion from here, resulting in mid- to high-teens EPS growth," noted Sport.

BofA raised its FY26-FY27 EPS estimates by 3% and 14% to reflect an improved sales and margin trajectory on the expectation for leverage on higher sales, average unit retail opportunities, and efficiency initiatives that will more than offset investments to support growth.  The firm set a price objective of $68.00 on Victoria's Secret (VSCO).

Shares of Victoria's Secret (VSCO) jumped 4.4% in premarket action. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on VSCO is flashing Strong Buy.
